spec_match.rationale: Constructs a real VideoFrame with rotation (0/90/180/270) and flip ("none"/"horizontal"/"vertical"), probes f.rotation and f.flip, and falls back to a manual canvas transform if metadata is dropped — accurate to the spec's IDL.

interactive.rationale: Two selects plus a render button drive twin canvases (raw vs respecting), with the support pill flipping based on a real VideoFrame round-trip probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a side-by-side raw vs respecting render driven by metadata; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers rotation × flip cleanly but never demonstrates the spec's order-of-operations question (flip then rotate vs rotate then flip), nor the codedWidth↔displayWidth swap at 90/270 — both canvases are sized for the source so the swap is hidden.

references.rationale: see-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520 and the canonical spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, accent-emerald/rose used with --bg-ivory text for contrast, no static-card. drawSourceTo uses raw hex inside canvas pixels which is fine since they are inside a bitmap, not CSS.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate order-of-operations (flip then rotate)
openQuestions.detail: The spec composes flip before rotate. The page applies flip after rotate in the canvas fallback (octx.rotate then octx.scale), which produces different output for flip=horizontal + rotation=90 than the spec intends. Either reorder the calls so the fallback matches spec, or add a toggle that exposes the difference so visitors learn it.

openQuestions.title: Surface displayWidth / displayHeight swap at 90/270
openQuestions.detail: The page reads f.rotation and f.flip but ignores frame.displayWidth and frame.displayHeight, which swap relative to codedWidth/codedHeight when rotation is 90 or 270. Adding a readout of all four dimensions makes the rotation-vs-coded-pixels point concrete.

openQuestions.title: Add an `Android front camera` preset
openQuestions.detail: The motivating real-world case is the Android front camera, which produces sensor-landscape + horizontal flip + 90 rotation. A one-click preset would tee up the canonical scenario without users having to find the combination.

openQuestions.title: Test the VideoEncoder active-orientation lock
openQuestions.detail: The spec requires VideoEncoder to throw DataError if a subsequent frame's rotation/flip differs from the first encoded frame's. This page doesn't touch the encoder path — leave that to the round-trip sibling — but a small `Try changing orientation mid-stream` button would teach the constraint.

summary: Solid live constructor demo with a real round-trip support probe. The biggest miss is the flip-then-rotate ordering — the fallback transforms in the wrong order — followed by ignoring displayWidth/displayHeight, which is the most visible spec consequence of rotation.
